prompt: Enum.valueOf throws IllegalArgumentException. What is the bug?
choices: valueOf is not defined on enums; use Enum.parse() instead
valueOf performs a case-sensitive lookup; "active" does not match the declared constant "ACTIVE", so it throws IllegalArgumentException
Enum constants cannot be retrieved by name at runtime
valueOf requires the enum class as the first argument: Status.valueOf(Status.class, "active")
explanation: Enum.valueOf() does an exact, case-sensitive match against the declared constant names. "active" != "ACTIVE", so it throws IllegalArgumentException. Fix: Status.valueOf("ACTIVE") or convert the input first: Status.valueOf(input.toUpperCase()).
